Restaurant Manager Job at Summerset Continental Hotel




        
    

        
        Welcome to Summerset Continental Hotel Abuja! Located at the prestigious address of 73 and 34 Usuma Street in Maitama, Abuja, our hotel is a haven of luxury and comfort. With a range of exquisite room categories, including Superior rooms, Executive rooms, and Premier suites, we offer accommodations tailored to meet the needs of discerning travelers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Restaurant Manager

Job Location: Abuja

Requirements
     

            
    




	?Background in continental and local cuisine.
	?Minimum of 2 years of experience in a reputed hotel or restaurant.
	?Must be Nigerian.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: [email protected] using the Job Positon as the sucject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Lead (New Business Models)",
  "description": "Roche is a global pioneer in pharmaceuticals and diagnostics focused on advancing science to improve people&rsquo;s lives. The combined strengths of pharmaceuticals and diagnostics under one roof have made Roche the leader in personalised healthcare &ndash; a strategy that aims to fit the right treatment to each patient in the best way possible. Roche is the world&rsquo;s largest biotech company, with truly differentiated medicines in oncology, immunology, infectious diseases, ophthalmology and diseases of the central nervous system. Roche is also the world leader in in vitro diagnostics and tissue-based cancer diagnostics, and a frontrunner in diabetes management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Lead (New Business Models)

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	As the Business Development Lead, you will own the end-to-end success (strategic, operational, and commercial) of the pilot in Morocco and the development of related models in Africa.
	Your primary mission is to drive the successful launch of the defined and validated business model, ensuring the business is commercially sustainable and scalable.
	This is an execution-focused role, responsible for bringing this innovative business model&rsquo;s vision to life.


Your Mission: A Phased Approach to Impact
Your role will evolve through three distinct phases:


	Execute the Blueprint: Lead the inaugural pilot implementation in Morocco to establish a successful, sustainable operational foundation. This includes commercial accountability for the pilot and its expansion
	Codify &amp; Deploy the Playbook: Ensure the systematic capture of insights and learnings from the Morocco pilot to create a replicable, scalable playbook for future deployments in Morocco and new markets
	Drive Replication: Leverage your practical expertise to support the scaling of other new business models across Subregion Africa, ensuring lessons from the pilot are integrated into future launches. This includes commercial accountability for future deployment


Key Responsibilities
Phase 1: Pilot Leadership (Morocco):


	Lead the on-the-ground implementation and operational scaling of the innovative model in Morocco
	Drive all commercial and contracting processes with laboratory network leaders to ensure sustainable deployment and financial viability
	Engage Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) and lab stakeholders to co-build the network, shape governance frameworks, and refine business models
	Collaborate with internal experts (e.g., legal, finance, procurement) and external partners to ensure a compliant and sustainable deployment of the model


Phase 2 &amp; 3: Playbook Development &amp; Replication Support:


	Systematically translate pilot execution into a practical, replicable success model for other regions
	Develop and empower regional and local teams through on-field coaching and guidance, building implementation expertise across the organization
	Serve as the subject matter expert on the Model&rsquo;s implementation, supporting the rollout of similar business models across public and private sectors in the sub-region


Candidate Profile: The Entrepreneurial Leader:


	You are a strategic and entrepreneurial leader with a proven ability to translate vision into concrete, on-the-ground execution.
	You thrive on operational challenges and excel at bringing complex projects to life.


Required Experience &amp; Qualifications


	A minimum of 5 years in the In Vitro Diagnostics (IVD) industry with significant exposure to market and business development
	A proven track record of translating high-level models or strategies into tangible, operational reality
	Strong financial acumen with deep experience in contracting, commercial negotiations, and managing financial viability within the healthcare sector
	Experience in both B2B and B2C environments is beneficial; direct experience with the Moroccan market is highly preferred
	Fluency in both English and French (spoken and written) is highly desirable.


Skills &amp; Mindset:


	Leadership: Strong people management skills with demonstrated success leading matrixed teams and influencing without direct authority to achieve impactful outcomes
	Stakeholder Engagement: Skilled at engaging C-suite stakeholders, building partnerships, and fostering trust across complex networks
	Problem-Solving: An entrepreneurial, proactive, and creative problem-solver who can overcome challenges and adapt solutions to different market contexts
	Communication: An exceptional oral and written communicator with strong business presentation skills.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-07-01",
  "validThrough": "2026-08-02",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Roche Nigeria"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "201261"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Business Development Lead (New Business Models) Job at Roche Nigeria




        
    

        
        Roche is a global pioneer in pharmaceuticals and diagnostics focused on advancing science to improve people’s lives. The combined strengths of pharmaceuticals and diagnostics under one roof have made Roche the leader in personalised healthcare – a strategy that aims to fit the right treatment to each patient in the best way possible. Roche is the world’s largest biotech company, with truly differentiated medicines in oncology, immunology, infectious diseases, ophthalmology and diseases of the central nervous system. Roche is also the world leader in in vitro diagnostics and tissue-based cancer diagnostics, and a frontrunner in diabetes management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

            
    



Job Position: Business Development Lead (New Business Models)

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	As the Business Development Lead, you will own the end-to-end success (strategic, operational, and commercial) of the pilot in Morocco and the development of related models in Africa.
	Your primary mission is to drive the successful launch of the defined and validated business model, ensuring the business is commercially sustainable and scalable.
	This is an execution-focused role, responsible for bringing this innovative business model’s vision to life.


Your Mission: A Phased Approach to Impact
Your role will evolve through three distinct phases:


	Execute the Blueprint: Lead the inaugural pilot implementation in Morocco to establish a successful, sustainable operational foundation. This includes commercial accountability for the pilot and its expansion
	Codify & Deploy the Playbook: Ensure the systematic capture of insights and learnings from the Morocco pilot to create a replicable, scalable playbook for future deployments in Morocco and new markets
	Drive Replication: Leverage your practical expertise to support the scaling of other new business models across Subregion Africa, ensuring lessons from the pilot are integrated into future launches. This includes commercial accountability for future deployment


Key Responsibilities
Phase 1: Pilot Leadership (Morocco):


	Lead the on-the-ground implementation and operational scaling of the innovative model in Morocco
	Drive all commercial and contracting processes with laboratory network leaders to ensure sustainable deployment and financial viability
	Engage Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) and lab stakeholders to co-build the network, shape governance frameworks, and refine business models
	Collaborate with internal experts (e.g., legal, finance, procurement) and external partners to ensure a compliant and sustainable deployment of the model


Phase 2 & 3: Playbook Development & Replication Support:


	Systematically translate pilot execution into a practical, replicable success model for other regions
	Develop and empower regional and local teams through on-field coaching and guidance, building implementation expertise across the organization
	Serve as the subject matter expert on the Model’s implementation, supporting the rollout of similar business models across public and private sectors in the sub-region


Candidate Profile: The Entrepreneurial Leader:


	You are a strategic and entrepreneurial leader with a proven ability to translate vision into concrete, on-the-ground execution.
	You thrive on operational challenges and excel at bringing complex projects to life.


Required Experience & Qualifications


	A minimum of 5 years in the In Vitro Diagnostics (IVD) industry with significant exposure to market and business development
	A proven track record of translating high-level models or strategies into tangible, operational reality
	Strong financial acumen with deep experience in contracting, commercial negotiations, and managing financial viability within the healthcare sector
	Experience in both B2B and B2C environments is beneficial; direct experience with the Moroccan market is highly preferred
	Fluency in both English and French (spoken and written) is highly desirable.


Skills & Mindset:


	Leadership: Strong people management skills with demonstrated success leading matrixed teams and influencing without direct authority to achieve impactful outcomes
	Stakeholder Engagement: Skilled at engaging C-suite stakeholders, building partnerships, and fostering trust across complex networks
	Problem-Solving: An entrepreneurial, proactive, and creative problem-solver who can overcome challenges and adapt solutions to different market contexts
	Communication: An exceptional oral and written communicator with strong business presentation skills.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",

  "title": "Internal Controller Officer",
  "description": "Lily hospitals Ltd, established since 1986 remains one of the foremost hospitals in Nigeria and within the South-South region of the country. It is the first private hospital in Nigeria to achieve ISO 9001:2000 quality management system certification from the Standards Organization of Nigeria (SON). Also we areconsistently committed to high standards; continuous improvement, service excellence, organizational citizenship, and outstanding work ethics.

At Lily hospitals, weare consistently committed to high standards; continuous improvement, service excellence, organizational citizenship, and outstanding work ethics. running residency training programs in family medicine and radiology. Lily Hospitals is also involved in housemanship training, internship training in Pharmacy and Radiology. We have multiple locations in Benin, Warri, Okuokoko etc.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Controller Officer&nbsp;

Job Location: Egbeda, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Workplace Type: Onsite
Job Function: Non-Clinical.

Job Description


	As the Internal Control Officer, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the effectiveness of our internal controls and processes. You will be responsible for assessing, monitoring, and enhancing the internal control environment to mitigate risks and safeguard company assets.


Your duties will include but are not limited to:


	Conducting regular evaluations of existing internal controls to identify areas for improvement.
	Developing and implementing control policies, procedures, and guidelines.
	Performing risk assessments to identify potential risks and vulnerabilities.
	Monitoring compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.
	Investigating incidents of fraud, misconduct, or non-compliance.
	Providing guidance and training to staff on internal control best practices.
	Collaborating with other departments to streamline processes and improve efficiency.
	Preparing reports and presentations for management on internal control findings and recommendations.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related fields
	Proven experience (2 - 5 years) in internal control, compliance, or related field, preferably in the Health or Service sector.
	Membership of relevant professional bodies such as the institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria (ICAN), ACCA will be an added advantage.
	Strong understanding of internal control concepts, frameworks, and methodologies.
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to communicate effectively with stakeholders at all levels.
	Attention to detail and a commitment to accuracy.
	Knowledge of COSO framework is required.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr@carepointhospitals.com using &quot;Internal Control Officer&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

Internal Controller Officer Job at Lily hospitals Limited




        
    

        
        Lily hospitals Ltd, established since 1986 remains one of the foremost hospitals in Nigeria and within the South-South region of the country. It is the first private hospital in Nigeria to achieve ISO 9001:2000 quality management system certification from the Standards Organization of Nigeria (SON). Also we areconsistently committed to high standards; continuous improvement, service excellence, organizational citizenship, and outstanding work ethics.
     

            
    



At Lily hospitals, weare consistently committed to high standards; continuous improvement, service excellence, organizational citizenship, and outstanding work ethics. running residency training programs in family medicine and radiology. Lily Hospitals is also involved in housemanship training, internship training in Pharmacy and Radiology. We have multiple locations in Benin, Warri, Okuokoko etc.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Controller Officer 

Job Location: Egbeda, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Workplace Type: Onsite
Job Function: Non-Clinical.

Job Description


	As the Internal Control Officer, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the effectiveness of our internal controls and processes. You will be responsible for assessing, monitoring, and enhancing the internal control environment to mitigate risks and safeguard company assets.


Your duties will include but are not limited to:


	Conducting regular evaluations of existing internal controls to identify areas for improvement.
	Developing and implementing control policies, procedures, and guidelines.
	Performing risk assessments to identify potential risks and vulnerabilities.
	Monitoring compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.
	Investigating incidents of fraud, misconduct, or non-compliance.
	Providing guidance and training to staff on internal control best practices.
	Collaborating with other departments to streamline processes and improve efficiency.
	Preparing reports and presentations for management on internal control findings and recommendations.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related fields
	Proven experience (2 - 5 years) in internal control, compliance, or related field, preferably in the Health or Service sector.
	Membership of relevant professional bodies such as the institute of Chartered Accountants of Nigeria (ICAN), ACCA will be an added advantage.
	Strong understanding of internal control concepts, frameworks, and methodologies.
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to communicate effectively with stakeholders at all levels.
	Attention to detail and a commitment to accuracy.
	Knowledge of COSO framework is required.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Internal Control Officer" as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Content Creator (Design & Lifestyle)",
  "description": "At Elvaridah, we emphasize on the critical matters and prospects of organizations such as business growth, strategy, planning, marketing, structure, and people management across different industries. We help companies focus on the growth of their businesses while aligning costs with strategy. With our all-inclusive approach and expertise, we capture values across the boundaries of any organization. Elvaridah focuses on optimizing all the different parts of a business as a unit.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Content Creator (Design &amp; Lifestyle)

Job Location: Anthony, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time (Hybrid)

About the Role


	We are seeking a creative and detail-oriented Content Creator with a strong passion for storytelling, design, and lifestyle.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for developing engaging and visually appealing content that reflects our brand&rsquo;s aesthetic and communicates the artistry behind our interior design projects.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop compelling written, visual, and video content for social media, website, and marketing campaigns.
	Tell engaging stories that bring our design projects to life &mdash; from concept to completion.
	Manage social media pages, ensuring consistent posting, tone, and visual appeal.
	Collaborate with designers and the marketing team to create project highlight videos, design features, and client case stories.
	Write blog posts, captions, and newsletters that reflect the brand&rsquo;s voice and style.
	Stay up to date with design trends, storytelling techniques, and social media best practices.
	Monitor content performance and suggest creative improvements.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Communications, Marketing, English, or a related field.
	At least 2 years of experience as a Content Creator, Copywriter, or Social Media Executive (experience in the design, lifestyle, or luxury sector is an advantage).
	Strong storytelling ability &mdash; both visual and written.
	Proficiency in social media management and content planning tools.
	Basic photo and video editing skills (Canva, CapCut, Adobe Suite, or similar).
	Excellent command of English and a good eye for aesthetics.
	Ability to work independently, meet deadlines, and maintain brand consistency.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications and their porfolios to: careers@elvaridah.com using the Job Positon as the subject of the mail.",

Content Creator (Design & Lifestyle) Job at Elvaridah




        
    

        
        At Elvaridah, we emphasize on the critical matters and prospects of organizations such as business growth, strategy, planning, marketing, structure, and people management across different industries. We help companies focus on the growth of their businesses while aligning costs with strategy. With our all-inclusive approach and expertise, we capture values across the boundaries of any organization. Elvaridah focuses on optimizing all the different parts of a business as a unit.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Content Creator (Design & Lifestyle)

Job Location: Anthony, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time (Hybrid)

About the Role
     

            
    




	We are seeking a creative and detail-oriented Content Creator with a strong passion for storytelling, design, and lifestyle.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for developing engaging and visually appealing content that reflects our brand’s aesthetic and communicates the artistry behind our interior design projects.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop compelling written, visual, and video content for social media, website, and marketing campaigns.
	Tell engaging stories that bring our design projects to life — from concept to completion.
	Manage social media pages, ensuring consistent posting, tone, and visual appeal.
	Collaborate with designers and the marketing team to create project highlight videos, design features, and client case stories.
	Write blog posts, captions, and newsletters that reflect the brand’s voice and style.
	Stay up to date with design trends, storytelling techniques, and social media best practices.
	Monitor content performance and suggest creative improvements.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Communications, Marketing, English, or a related field.
	At least 2 years of experience as a Content Creator, Copywriter, or Social Media Executive (experience in the design, lifestyle, or luxury sector is an advantage).
	Strong storytelling ability — both visual and written.
	Proficiency in social media management and content planning tools.
	Basic photo and video editing skills (Canva, CapCut, Adobe Suite, or similar).
	Excellent command of English and a good eye for aesthetics.
	Ability to work independently, meet deadlines, and maintain brand consistency.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications and their porfolios to: [email protected] using the Job Positon as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Mechanical Engineer",
  "description": "Adkan Service Nigeria Limited is a wholly indigenous construction company with a bias of undertaking construction projects essentially within the country. The reason for this nationalistic and patriotic bent is to join hands with others of like minds in other specialised fields in making Nigeria not only the pride of other nations but a reference point in Architectural designs and brilliantly executed construction projects. In furtherance of this mission statement, it is worth stating that all our completed projects can stand the test of time and can also compete favourably with similar projects elsewhere in the world.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mechanical Engineer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a competent and experienced Mechanical Engineer to design, supervise, and manage mechanical systems within construction and engineering projects.
	The ideal candidate will ensure efficient system performance, compliance with standards, and successful project delivery.


Key Responsibilities


	Design and oversee installation of mechanical systems (HVAC, plumbing, fire protection, etc.)
	Prepare mechanical drawings, specifications, and technical reports
	Review project designs to ensure feasibility and compliance with standards
	Supervise on-site mechanical works and ensure adherence to design specifications
	Conduct inspections, testing, and commissioning of mechanical systems
	Troubleshoot and resolve mechanical issues during project execution
	Ensure compliance with safety regulations and engineering standards
	Collaborate with structural, electrical, and project teams

Mechanical Engineer Job at Adkan Service Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Adkan Service Nigeria Limited is a wholly indigenous construction company with a bias of undertaking construction projects essentially within the country. The reason for this nationalistic and patriotic bent is to join hands with others of like minds in other specialised fields in making Nigeria not only the pride of other nations but a reference point in Architectural designs and brilliantly executed construction projects. In furtherance of this mission statement, it is worth stating that all our completed projects can stand the test of time and can also compete favourably with similar projects elsewhere in the world.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mechanical Engineer
     

            
    



Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a competent and experienced Mechanical Engineer to design, supervise, and manage mechanical systems within construction and engineering projects.
	The ideal candidate will ensure efficient system performance, compliance with standards, and successful project delivery.


Key Responsibilities


	Design and oversee installation of mechanical systems (HVAC, plumbing, fire protection, etc.)
	Prepare mechanical drawings, specifications, and technical reports
	Review project designs to ensure feasibility and compliance with standards
	Supervise on-site mechanical works and ensure adherence to design specifications
	Conduct inspections, testing, and commissioning of mechanical systems
	Troubleshoot and resolve mechanical issues during project execution
	Ensure compliance with safety regulations and engineering standards
	Collaborate with structural, electrical, and project teams
	Monitor project timelines and provide progress reports.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree (B.Sc. / HND) in Mechanical Engineering or related fields.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in mechanical engineering within construction or related industry (mandatory).
	Strong knowledge of mechanical systems and building services
	Proficiency in engineering design software (e.g., AutoCAD, Revit MEP, or similar tools)
	Good understanding of installation, testing, and maintenance procedures
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
	Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.


Preferred Qualifications:


	Professional registration or membership (e.g., COREN, NSE)
	Experience working on residential, commercial, or industrial projects
	Familiarity with Nigerian engineering standards and regulations
	Experience in project supervision and coordination.


Additional Requirements:


	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	High level of attention to detail and accuracy
	Strong organizational and time management skills
	High level of professionalism and integrity.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV, copies of professional certifications, and relevant credentials to: [email protected]m using "Mechanical Engineer" as the subject of the email.
